# nordic-csp-engine

## Table of Contents

- [nordic-csp-engine](#nordic-csp-engine)
  - [Table of Contents](#table-of-contents)
  - [Description](#description)
  - [Usage](#usage)
    - [Install](#install)
    - [Tests](#tests)
  - [Contributing](#contributing)
  - [Changelog](#changelog)

## Description

The `nordic-csp-engine` module simplifies the generation of a Content Security Policy (CSP) header for Nordic applications. It uses the **SCOPE** and **application name** to fetch the appropriate configuration from the CSP API provided by the WebSec team. This ensures that your Nordic application has a properly configured CSP to enhance security and mitigate potential risks.

This module has three main functionalities:

- `csp-middleware`: An Express middleware that handles every request and adds the CSP header to the response based on a given configuration. It also sets the `nonce` in the response `locals`.
- `csp-config`: A function that runs every 5 minutes and retrieves the configurations from the WebSec API. It sets the retrieved configuration in memory to be used by the middleware.
- `security-config`: A function that runs periodically to retrieve security configurations including input validation settings, CSRF protection rules, CSP trusted types policies, and CSP strict policy configurations (for backend endpoints) from the WebSec API.

Additionally, the module includes the `cron` module to facilitate periodic configuration updates. The `cron` module manages all the necessary configuration to run a Cron Job, either locally or in Fury, following the cluster model from [ragnar](https://github.com/melisource/fury_frontend-ragnar).

## Usage

1. Add the middleware to your Express app:
   > Note: The Cron job will run in the moment you require `nordic-csp-engine`.

```js
import { cspMiddleware } from 'nordic-csp-engine';

app.use(cspMiddleware());
```

2. Access the cron storage to retrieve stored configurations from other modules:

```js
import { websecConfig } from 'nordic-csp-engine';

// Get stored security configuration
const securityConfig = await websecConfig.get('securityConfig');
```

3. Change the `SCOPE` variable in your local environment to test different configurations.

```sh
export SCOPE=local
```

## The Cron adaptation

> Note: The `cron` module is meant to be used internally in the Nordic stack.

The `cron` module is a wrapper around the `node-cron` module. It provides a simple interface to run a Cron job in a Node cluster environment. The `cron` module will run the job in the master process and will send a message to all the workers to update their configuration in the `CronStorage`. This ensures that all workers have an updated configuration to use in any given moment in the same process, for example, the `csp-middleware`.

### Main functionalities

- `CronManager`: a node-cron wrapper as a class that seeks to administrate all the Cron jobs on an application having the ability to add a new job or remove, start and stop any job.

**Example**:

```js
import cronManager from './lib/cron/manager';

const cronConfig = {
  name: 'test',
  cronTime: '*/5 * * * * *',
  handler: () => console.log('test'),
};

cronManager.addJob(cronConfig);
cronManager.addJob(cronConfig);
cronManager.addJob(cronConfig);
cronManager.start();
```

> Note: The `cronManager` will execute the handler function when the start method is called. This is meant for all those handler that have a long execution time.

- `CronStorage`: In memory singleton storage where all the data retrieved by the handlers are stored, this is a single instance for each process, in the cluster model, all the workers and the master have a different instance but with the same data.

**Example**:

```js
import cronStorage from './lib/cron/storage';

cronStorage.set('test', 'test');
cronStorage.get('test');
// test
```

- `Cluster Adapter`: a wrapper for the `cron` module. It is designed to handle Cron jobs in both local and production environments. In the production environment, the Cron job is executed in the master process, while in the local environment, it is executed in the same process. This approach ensures that the Cron job handlers are not executed in multiple processes, preventing duplicated execution. The `Cluster Adapter` also handles sending messages to all the workers to update their configuration in the `CronStorage`.

**Example**:

```js
import { initCronJobs } from './adapter';

initCronJobs();
```

### Install

```sh
npm install --save nordic-csp-engine
```

### Tests

```sh
npm test
```
